messages.content:
Classify this job. Respond with exactly one JSON object, no extra text.

loc: IN_LONDON | REMOTE | UK_OTHER | OUTSIDE_UK | UNK
  Use the Location field only. Ignore location hints in the description.
  "Anywhere" → REMOTE. "Fully Remote" or "Remote" → REMOTE.
  Any non-UK country → OUTSIDE_UK (even if "Remote" appears). E.g. "Paris, France (Remote)" → OUTSIDE_UK.
  "Greater London", "London Area", "London, England" → IN_LONDON. "Greater Manchester" → UK_OTHER.
  UK cities: Manchester, Birmingham, Leeds, Bristol, Edinburgh, Glasgow, Cambridge, Oxford, Brighton, Sheffield, Liverpool, Newcastle, Nottingham, Cardiff, Belfast, Southampton, Reading, York, Bath, Coventry, Leicester, Aberdeen, Dundee, Swansea, Hull, Plymouth, Exeter, Norwich, Ipswich, Portsmouth, Bournemouth, Chester, Derby, Milton Keynes, Cheltenham, Basingstoke, Guildford, Warwick → UK_OTHER.
  "United Kingdom" or "UK" or "England" or "Scotland" or "Wales" without a specific city → UK_OTHER.
  No location → UNK.

arr: REMOTE | HYBRID | IN_OFFICE | UNK
  REMOTE: explicitly remote-only or remote-first, no required office days.
  HYBRID: mix of office and remote (e.g. "hybrid", "2–3 days a week in office").
  IN_OFFICE: clearly expects in-person work with no remote option.
  UNK: arrangement not specified.

sen: LEVEL_3 | LEVEL_2 | LEVEL_1
  Decide using the job TITLE only. Ignore experience language in the description.
  L3: senior, lead, staff, principal, head, director, VP, CTO, founding, distinguished, sr, III, manager, mid-senior, mid/senior. Any title containing "manager" → L3.
  L1: junior, jr, graduate, intern, trainee, apprentice, associate, I (roman numeral), internship.
  L2: everything else, including II. E.g. "Technical Writer" → LEVEL_2.

tech: subset of [NODE, REACT, JS_TS, AI_ML], or exactly ["OOS"] when none apply.
  Include a token if that technology appears ANYWHERE in the JD, even as a secondary skill.
  NODE = Node.js runtime only. Next.js is NOT Node.js → JS_TS. NestJS is NOT Node.js → JS_TS.
  AI_ML = any mention of AI, ML, machine learning, LLM, generative AI, deep learning.
  JS_TS = JavaScript or TypeScript mentioned in the tech stack.
  REACT = React, React Native, or React.js.
  ["OOS"] only when NONE of the above appear anywhere in the JD.
  NEVER mix "OOS" with other tokens. NEVER use empty arrays or empty strings.

comp: NO_GBP | UP_TO_ONLY | BELOW_45K | RANGE_45_54K | RANGE_55_74K | RANGE_75_99K | ABOVE_100K
  ONLY £ or "GBP" counts. $, USD, EUR, CAD, AUD, daily rates → NO_GBP. OTE amounts → NO_GBP.
  No salary → NO_GBP.
  "Up to £X" or "to £X" with no lower bound → UP_TO_ONLY.
  For ranges: use the MIDPOINT. £60k–£80k → midpoint £70k → RANGE_55_74K. £75k–£100k → midpoint £87.5k → RANGE_75_99K.
  Multiple bands listed → use the lowest band.

Title: Cloud Solutions Architect
Location: Cambridge, UK
Description: About Us:
Vertex Technologies specialise in delivering cloud transformation projects for the life sciences sector.

The Role:
We are looking for a Cloud Solutions Architect with expertise in Azure and GCP to lead design and implementation of cloud infrastructures.

Requirements:
- Extensive experience designing cloud solutions using Azure and GCP
- Understanding of security best practices and compliance
- Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
- Experience with containerisation and orchestration (Kubernetes)
- Background in software development an advantage

Salary & Benefits:
Competitive salary up to £85k
Flexible working hours and remote work
Health care and pension
Professional development support

How to Apply:
Send CV and cover letter to recruitment@vertextech.co.uk.

Begin your response with {

{"loc_raw":"Cambridge, UK","loc":"UK_OTHER","arr_raw":"Flexible working hours and remote work","arr":"HYBRID","sen_raw":"Cloud Solutions Architect","sen":"LEVEL_3","tech_raw":"Azure, GCP, Kubernetes","tech":["OOS"],"comp_raw":"Competitive salary up to £85k","comp":"UP_TO_ONLY"}

Classify this job. Respond with exactly one JSON object, no extra text.

  "United Kingdom" or "UK" or "England" or "Scotland" or "Wales" without a specific city → UK_OTHER.

  L2: everything else, including II. E.g. "Technical Writer" → LEVEL_2.

  Include a token if that technology appears ANYWHERE in the JD, even as a secondary skill.
  NODE = Node.js runtime only. Next.js is NOT Node.js → JS_TS. NestJS is NOT Node.js → JS_TS.
  AI_ML = any mention of AI, ML, machine learning, LLM, generative AI, deep learning.
  JS_TS = JavaScript or TypeScript mentioned in the tech stack.
  REACT = React, React Native, or React.js.
  ["OOS"] only when NONE of the above appear anywhere in the JD.
  NEVER mix "OOS" with other tokens. NEVER use empty arrays or empty strings.

  ONLY £ or "GBP" counts. $, USD, EUR, CAD, AUD, daily rates → NO_GBP. OTE amounts → NO_GBP.
  No salary → NO_GBP.
  "Up to £X" or "to £X" with no lower bound → UP_TO_ONLY.
  For ranges: use the MIDPOINT. £60k–£80k → midpoint £70k → RANGE_55_74K. £75k–£100k → midpoint £87.5k → RANGE_75_99K.
  Multiple bands listed → use the lowest band.

Title: Senior Backend Engineer (Node.js)
Location: London, UK
Description: About Us:
StratoWave Technologies is a leading provider of cloud-based logistics solutions, committed to transforming supply chain management through innovative backend systems. We pride ourselves on fostering a collaborative and dynamic environment where bold ideas thrive.

The Role:
We are seeking a Senior Backend Engineer skilled in Node.js to join our growing engineering team. You will be responsible for designing and implementing high-performance APIs and microservices that power our logistics platform. Your focus will be on building scalable backend solutions using Node.js and Express, integrating with PostgreSQL and Redis for data management and caching.

Responsibilities include:
- Developing RESTful APIs and backend services
- Collaborating with cross-functional teams to define system architecture
- Maintaining and improving existing codebases
- Optimizing database queries and system performance
- Implementing containerization with Docker and deploying to AWS environments

Requirements:
- 5+ years of experience in backend development with a strong focus on Node.js
- Proficiency with Express framework
- Experience with relational databases, preferably PostgreSQL
- Familiarity with Redis or other caching technologies
- Good understanding of containerization (Docker) and cloud deployment (AWS)
- Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

Salary & Benefits:
- £65,000 - £80,000 per annum
- Flexible working hours and remote work options
- Private healthcare and pension plan
- Annual training budget and conference attendance

How to Apply:
Please submit your CV and a cover letter detailing your relevant experience to careers@stratowave.co.uk with the subject line 'Senior Backend Engineer Application'.

Begin your response with {

{"loc_raw":"London, UK","loc":"IN_LONDON","arr_raw":"Flexible working hours and remote work options","arr":"HYBRID","sen_raw":"Senior Backend Engineer (Node.js)","sen":"LEVEL_3","tech_raw":"Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker, AWS","tech":["NODE"],"comp_raw":"£65,000 - £80,000 per annum","comp":"RANGE_55_74K"}

Title: Full Stack AI Developer - Node.js & React
Location: London, UK
Description: About Us:
NeuraLogic builds AI-powered SaaS solutions.

The Role:
Develop full stack AI applications using Node.js and React.

Responsibilities:
- Backend and frontend development
- AI model integration

Requirements:
- Node.js, React skills
- AI/ML experience

Salary/Benefits:
- £60,000 - £75,000

How to Apply:
Email hr@neuralogic.com.
